response.justification: Bogdan is a highly accomplished full-stack software engineer with 20+ years of experience and demonstrated expertise in performance optimization and infrastructure design. However, this Principal Performance Architect role requires deep, specialized expertise in hardware architecture, SoC and IP modeling, GPU optimization, and silicon-level hardware-software co-design—domains entirely absent from his resume. His background is fundamentally software-centric, with no evidence of chip design, hardware architecture work, SIMD/MIMD systems, or deep learning inference/training optimization. The domain shift required is too significant for a Principal-level hire.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering experience with demonstrated skill in performance optimization, low-latency distributed systems, and handling high-throughput workloads
Strong systems thinking evidenced by microservices architecture, real-time metadata processing, and device orchestration work
Current exposure to AI infrastructure and agentic workflows at Naver indicates emerging interest in AI-adjacent technical domains
concerns: No experience with SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture design—foundational requirements for this Principal role
Absence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD optimization, or any silicon-level hardware work; career is exclusively software-focused
No demonstrated expertise in deep neural network training/inference optimization, hardware-software co-design at the chip level, or EDA/design automation tools

key_factors.factor: Missing GPU and AI accelerator experience
Strong performance optimization and systems background
